findings.title: LiteLLM Pre-Auth SQL Injection Actively Exploited Within 36 Hours of Disclosure
findings.summary: Pre-auth SQL injection in LiteLLM's API key verification path is being actively exploited by attackers enumerating provider secrets (OpenAI/Anthropic/Bedrock) directly from the database; patched in 1.83.7.

findings.title: GitHub Enterprise Server Authenticated-Push RCE — 88% of GHE Instances Vulnerable at Disclosure
findings.affected_technology: GitHub.com, GitHub Enterprise Cloud, GitHub Enterprise Server 3.14-3.20
findings.summary: Wiz disclosed a command injection in GitHub's git push pipeline where any authenticated user with push access can execute arbitrary commands as the git user, with ~88% of GHE instances vulnerable at coordinated disclosure.

findings.title: Hugging Face LeRobot Unauthenticated Pickle Deserialization RCE — Currently Unpatched
findings.summary: LeRobot deserializes pickle payloads on unauthenticated plaintext gRPC channels, allowing any network-reachable attacker to execute code on the policy server or robot client; vendor fix planned for v0.6.0.

findings.title: CoreDNS TSIG Authentication Bypass Bundle Across DoH/DoT/DoQ/QUIC/gRPC Transports
findings.summary: Five CoreDNS advisories on 2026-04-28 include TSIG signature verification bypasses on gRPC/QUIC and on DoT/DoH/DoH3/DoQ/gRPC, plus subzone ACL bypass and DoS amplification, all fixed in 1.14.3.

findings.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202 by APT28
findings.summary: Microsoft revised the advisory on 2026-04-27 to confirm active exploitation; Akamai attributed the LNK-coercion + MSHTML chain to APT28 targeting Ukraine and EU entities, raising urgency for organisations not yet on April Patch Tuesday.

noted.product: Microsoft Entra ID — Agent ID Administrator role
noted.summary: Silverfort disclosed an Entra ID role flaw allowing arbitrary service principal takeover; Microsoft patched 2026-04-09, no active exploitation reported.
Local privilege escalation via fake RPC servers impersonating disabled Windows services; Microsoft classified moderate and declined to patch — out of remote-services scope.
Command injection bypass via underscore-form Python kwargs (upload_pack=, receive_pack=) sneaking past safety check before dashification (CVSS 8.8); fixed in 3.1.47.
Critical CVSS 10.0 unauthenticated RCE in consumer router firmware — out of corporate scope but a likely Mirai/IoT botnet candidate.

Unauthenticated authorization bypass (CVSS 9.8) and FOBS deserialization RCE (CVSS 8.8) in NVIDIA federated learning framework — niche deployment but the unauth-bypass + RCE pairing is the FastGPT/Langflow template.
Two new Spring AI advisories (CVSS 8.8 / 6.5) published 2026-04-28; details pending — promote next cycle if they intersect with the Spring Boot actuator bypass covered 2026-04-28.

ShinyHunters used stolen Anodot SaaS authentication tokens to pivot into customer Snowflake/BigQuery instances; video metadata and some customer emails exposed (no video content / credentials / payment data).
Energy/water meter vendor disclosed unauthorised access detected 2026-04-13; preliminary, no attribution, no data details, no operational impact reported.

noted.product: Signal phishing campaign vs German federal officials
noted.summary: German federal prosecutors investigating sustained phishing of senior officials' Signal accounts attributed to Russian state actors since February 2026 — operational/political signal.

noted.product: ClickFix campaign disguised as Claude installer (Rapid7)
noted.summary: ClickFix social engineering luring users to fake Claude installer pages that run PowerShell payloads; latest in fake AI-tool installer trend (mirrors Cursor/Windsurf installer phishing).

noted.product: VECT 2.0 ransomware (broken encryption acts as wiper)
noted.summary: VECT 2.0 has a fatal encryption flaw that destroys files >131KB on Windows/Linux/ESXi targets — files are unrecoverable, do not pay; push IOCs to AV/EDR.
Browser extension campaign harvested credentials from ~20,000 users — client-side, out of corporate-perimeter scope but worth noting if extension allow-list is loose.
